1→--- 2→description: Reconcile branch with remote and resolve divergence intelligently 3→--- 4→ 5→# Reconcile With Remote 6→ 7→Resolve "Branch X has been updated remotely" errors by reconciling with remote and handling any resulting conflicts. 8→ 9→## Steps 10→ 11→1. **Fetch remote state** 12→ 13→ ```bash 14→ git fetch origin 15→ ``` 16→ 17→2. **Get current branch name** 18→ 19→ ```bash 20→ BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) 21→ ``` 22→ 23→3. **Diagnose divergence** - Understand what changed on each side: 24→ 25→ ```bash 26→ # Commits only on remote (what we need to incorporate) 27→ git log --oneline HEAD..origin/$BRANCH 28→ 29→ # Commits only on local (our work that might be lost) 30→ git log --oneline origin/$BRANCH..HEAD
... [103 lines omitted] ...
42→ | 0 | 1+ | Already ahead (shouldn't trigger this error) | 84→ > Without this step, `gt restack` will fail with a "diverged from tracking" error because Graphite's cached SHAs no longer match the rebased commits.

1→"""Reconcile branch with remote. 2→ 3→Uses Claude to reconcile a diverged local branch with its remote tracking branch, 4→handling rebase and conflicts as needed. Invokes the /erk:reconcile-with-remote 5→Claude slash command. 6→""" 7→ 8→import click 9→ 10→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 11→from erk.cli.output import stream_reconcile_with_remote 12→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 13→ 14→ 15→@click.command("reconcile-with-remote") 16→@click.option( 17→ "-d", 18→ "--dangerous", 19→ is_flag=True, 20→ help="Acknowledge that this command invokes Claude with --dangerously-skip-permissions.", 21→) 22→@click.pass_obj 23→def pr_reconcile_with_remote(ctx: ErkContext, *, dangerous: bool) -> None: 24→ """Reconcile branch with remote and resolve divergence. 25→ 26→ When gt submit fails with "Branch has been updated remotely", this command 27→ fetches remote changes, analyzes divergence, rebases if needed, and resolves 28→ any conflicts using Claude. 29→ 30→ Examples:
... [67 lines omitted] ...
48→ raise click.ClickException("Not on a branch (detached HEAD)") 52→ raise click.ClickException(f"No remote tracking branch: origin/{current_branch}") 76→ raise click.ClickException( 87→ raise click.ClickException("Semantic decision requires interactive resolution") 89→ raise click.ClickException(result.error_message or "Reconcile with remote failed")

41→ """Format final summary box with status, PR link, timing, errors. 65→ lines.append(Text("❌ Status: Failed", style="red")) 108→ # Error details (if failed) 112→ if result.error_message: 114→ lines.append(Text(f"Error in command {i + 1}:", style="red bold")) 115→ lines.append(Text(result.error_message, style="red")) 121→ title = "Implementation Complete" if overall_success else "Implementation Failed" 148→ - Error events: ` ! error message` (red) 150→ - End (failure): `--- Failed (1m 23s) ---` (red) 179→ error_message: str | None = None 224→ case ErrorEvent(message=msg): 226→ error_message = msg 230→ error_message = diag 234→ error_message = diag 236→ case ProcessErrorEvent(message=msg): 238→ error_message = msg 252→ click.echo(click.style(f"--- Failed ({duration_str}) ---", fg="red", bold=True), err=True) 261→ error_message=error_message, 271→ error_message: str | None = None 292→ RebaseResult with success status and error details 294→ error_message: str | None = None 342→ case ErrorEvent(message=msg): 344→ error_message = msg 348→ error_message = diag 352→ error_message = diag 354→ case ProcessErrorEvent(message=msg): 356→ error_message = msg 361→ # Check for no-work-events failure mode 364→ error_message = ( 368→ click.echo(click.style(f" {error_message}", fg="yellow")) 378→ click.echo(click.style(f"--- Failed ({duration_str}) ---", fg="red", bold=True)) 380→ return RebaseResult(success=success, error_message=error_message) 388→ error_message: str | None = None 409→ ReconcileWithRemoteResult with success status and error details 411→ error_message: str | None = None 459→ case ErrorEvent(message=msg): 461→ error_message = msg 465→ error_message = diag 469→ error_message = diag 471→ case ProcessErrorEvent(message=msg): 473→ error_message = msg 478→ # Check for no-work-events failure mode 481→ error_message = ( 485→ click.echo(click.style(f" {error_message}", fg="yellow")) 495→ click.echo(click.style(f"--- Failed ({duration_str}) ---", fg="red", bold=True)) 497→ return ReconcileWithRemoteResult(success=success, error_message=error_message)

1→"""CLI error handling utilities with styled output. 2→ 3→This module provides the Ensure class for asserting invariants in CLI commands 4→with consistent, user-friendly error messages. All errors use red "Error:" prefix 5→for visual consistency. 6→ 7→Domain-Specific Methods: 8→- Git state validations (branch checks, worktree existence, clean state) 9→- Configuration validations (required fields, format checks) 10→- Argument validations (count, type, range) 11→- File/path validations (readable, writable, not hidden) 12→- String/collection validations (non-empty, non-null) 13→- External tool validations (gh CLI installed) 14→""" 15→ 16→from __future__ import annotations 17→ 18→import shutil 19→from pathlib import Path 20→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, TypeVar 21→ 22→import click 23→ 24→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.disabled import GraphiteDisabled, GraphiteDisabledError 25→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 26→ 27→if TYPE_CHECKING: 28→ from erk.core.context import ErkContext 29→ 30→T = TypeVar("T")
... [557 lines omitted] ...
33→class UserFacingCliError(click.ClickException): 34→ """Exception for user-facing CLI errors with styled output. 36→ Extends click.ClickException so Click catches it automatically at every 37→ level (groups, subgroups, commands) and converts it to a styled error 42→ raise UserFacingCliError("Not a GitHub repository") 43→ raise UserFacingCliError(push_result.message) 51→ """Display styled error message to stderr.""" 52→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + self.format_message()) 56→ """Helper class for asserting invariants with consistent error handling.""" 59→ def invariant(condition: bool, error_message: str) -> None: 60→ """Ensure condition is true, otherwise output styled error and exit. 64→ error_message: Error message to display if condition is false. 65→ "Error: " prefix will be added automatically in red. 68→ UserFacingCliError: If condition is false 71→ raise UserFacingCliError(error_message) 74→ def truthy(value: T, error_message: str) -> T: 75→ """Ensure value is truthy, otherwise output styled error and exit. 79→ error_message: Error message to display if value is falsy. 80→ "Error: " prefix will be added automatically in red. 86→ UserFacingCliError: If value is falsy 89→ raise UserFacingCliError(error_message) 93→ def not_none(value: T | None, error_message: str) -> T: 94→ """Ensure value is not None, otherwise output styled error and exit. 102→ error_message: Error message to display if value is None. 103→ "Error: " prefix will be added automatically in red. 109→ UserFacingCliError: If value is None 118→ raise UserFacingCliError(error_message) 133→ click.UsageError: If flag is required but not provided 143→ raise click.UsageError( 151→ error_message: str | None = None, 153→ """Ensure path exists, otherwise output styled error and exit. 164→ error_message: Optional custom error message. If not provided, 166→ "Error: " prefix will be added automatically in red. 169→ UserFacingCliError: If path does not exist 172→ >>> # Basic usage with default error message 175→ >>> # With custom error message 179→ if error_message is None: 180→ error_message = f"Path not found: {path}" 181→ raise UserFacingCliError(error_message) 184→ def not_empty(value: str | list | dict | None, error_message: str) -> None: 189→ error_message: Error message to display if value is empty. 190→ "Error: " prefix will be added automatically in red. 193→ UserFacingCliError: If value is None, empty string, empty list, or empty dict 200→ raise UserFacingCliError(error_message) 204→ """Ensure worktree exists at path, otherwise output styled error and exit. 209→ name: Optional worktree name for friendlier error message 216→ >>> Ensure.git_worktree_exists(ctx, wt_path) # Uses path in error 219→ error_message = f"Worktree '{name}' does not exist" 221→ error_message = f"Worktree not found: {wt_path}" 222→ Ensure.path_exists(ctx, wt_path, error_message) 226→ """Ensure git branch exists, otherwise output styled error and exit. 234→ UserFacingCliError: If branch does not exist 241→ raise UserFacingCliError( 247→ """Ensure currently in a git worktree, otherwise output styled error and exit. 250→ ctx: Application context (for error handling) 254→ UserFacingCliError: If not in a git worktree 261→ raise UserFacingCliError( 269→ error_message: str | None = None, 271→ """Ensure argument count matches expected, otherwise output styled error and exit. 276→ error_message: Optional custom error message 279→ UserFacingCliError: If argument count does not match expected 286→ if error_message is None: 288→ error_message = f"Expected no arguments, got {len(args)}" 290→ error_message = f"Expected 1 argument, got {len(args)}" 292→ error_message = f"Expected {expected} arguments, got {len(args)}" 293→ raise UserFacingCliError(error_message) 299→ error_message: str | None = None, 301→ """Ensure configuration field is set, otherwise output styled error and exit. 306→ error_message: Optional custom error message 309→ UserFacingCliError: If field is not set (None or missing) 320→ except AttributeError: 323→ except (KeyError, TypeError): 327→ if error_message is None: 328→ error_message = ( 332→ raise UserFacingCliError(error_message) 335→ def path_is_dir(ctx: ErkContext, path: Path, error_message: str | None = None) -> None: 336→ """Ensure path exists and is a directory, otherwise output styled error and exit. 341→ error_message: Optional custom error message 344→ UserFacingCliError: If path doesn't exist or is not a directory 349→ Ensure.path_exists(ctx, path, error_message) 351→ if error_message is None: 352→ error_message = f"Path is not a directory: {path}" 353→ raise UserFacingCliError(error_message) 356→ def path_not_exists(ctx: ErkContext, path: Path, error_message: str) -> None: 357→ """Ensure path does NOT exist, otherwise output styled error and exit. 364→ error_message: Error message to display if path exists 367→ UserFacingCliError: If path already exists 378→ raise UserFacingCliError(error_message) 388→ UserFacingCliError: If gh CLI is not found on PATH 396→ raise UserFacingCliError( 410→ UserFacingCliError: If gt CLI is not found on PATH 418→ raise UserFacingCliError( 429→ outputs a helpful error message based on why Graphite is unavailable 438→ UserFacingCliError: If Graphite is disabled or not installed 446→ error = GraphiteDisabledError(ctx.graphite.reason) 447→ raise UserFacingCliError(str(error)) 457→ UserFacingCliError: If claude CLI is not found on PATH 465→ raise UserFacingCliError( 482→ UserFacingCliError: If gt is not authenticated 492→ raise UserFacingCliError( 511→ UserFacingCliError: If gh is not installed or not authenticated 522→ raise UserFacingCliError( 544→ If branch exists and is NOT tracked, errors with remediation steps. 571→ # Branch exists but is not tracked - error with remediation 572→ raise UserFacingCliError(

1→"""Linear pipeline for PR submission. 2→ 3→Replaces the dual-path branching orchestration with a sequence of plain 4→functions transforming a frozen SubmitState dataclass. 5→ 6→Each step: (ErkContext, SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError 7→""" 8→ 9→import concurrent.futures 10→import dataclasses 11→import sys 12→import uuid 13→from collections.abc import Callable 14→from dataclasses import dataclass 15→from functools import cache 16→from pathlib import Path 17→ 18→import click 19→ 20→from erk.cli.commands.pr.shared import ( 21→ assemble_pr_body, 22→ echo_plan_context_status, 23→ maybe_advance_lifecycle_to_impl, 24→ recover_plan_header, 25→ run_commit_message_generation, 26→) 27→from erk.cli.constants import PLANNED_PR_TITLE_PREFIX 28→from erk.cli.ensure import UserFacingCliError 29→from erk.core.commit_message_generator import CommitMessageGenerator 30→from erk.core.context import ErkContext
... [1054 lines omitted] ...

" 427→ f"Error: {pull_result.message}

session.assistant:
Perfect! I now have all the full content of the 8 files you requested. Here's a comprehensive summary:
## Summary of `reconcile-with-remote` Implementation
### File Overview
1. **`.claude/commands/erk/reconcile-with-remote.md`** (Lines 1-128) - Command documentation with 10-step procedure for branch divergence resolution - Covers diagnosis (fast-forward vs rebase), conflict resolution, Graphite tracking updates - Includes edge case handling (force-push history rewrites, uncommitted changes, multi-branch stacks)
2. **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/reconcile_with_remote_cmd.py`** (Lines 1-92) - Click command implementation (`pr_reconcile_with_remote`) - Requires `--dangerous` flag (unless config disables) - Checks for valid branch and remote tracking, fetches remote, analyzes divergence - Invokes Claude via `/erk:reconcile-with-remote` command through `stream_reconcile_with_remote()`
3. **`tests/commands/pr/test_reconcile_with_remote.py`** (Lines 1-355) - 9 test cases covering: - Success path with divergence - Dangerous flag requirement/bypass via config - Already-in-sync branch (early exit) - Behind-only (fast-forward possible) - Error conditions: no remote branch, detached HEAD, Claude unavailable - Semantic conflicts requiring interactive resolution - Command execution failures - No-work-events failure mode
4. **`docs/learned/cli/commands/pr-reconcile-with-remote.md`** (Lines 1-84) - User documentation with usage, when to use, flags, and error conditions - Links to implementation details and related commands
5. **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/__init__.py`** (Lines 1-46) - PR command group registration - Imports `pr_reconcile_with_remote` and registers as `reconcile-with-remote` (Line 44)
6. **`src/erk/cli/output.py`** (Lines 266-498) - `ReconcileWithRemoteResult` dataclass (Lines 384-389): `success`, `error_message`, `requires_interactive` - `stream_reconcile_with_remote()` function (Lines 392-497): - Streams `/erk:reconcile-with-remote` command execution - Detects semantic conflicts via "AskUserQuestion" tool events - Handles no-work-events failure mode - Returns result with error details
7. **`src/erk/cli/ensure.py`** (Lines 122-146) - `Ensure.dangerous_flag()` method validates `--dangerous` requirement - Checks `ctx.global_config.require_dangerous_flag_for_implicitly_dangerous_operations` - Raises `UsageError` with config hint if flag required but not provided
8. **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py`** (Lines 279-305) - Remote divergence pre-check in `_graphite_first_flow()` - Auto-detects divergence before `gt submit` - Returns `SubmitError` with remediation message: ``` erk pr reconcile-with-remote --dangerous erk pr submit -f ```
---
## Key Implementation Details for Rename `reconcile-with-remote` → `diverge-fix`
### Naming Pattern - **Command name**: `reconcile-with-remote` → `diverge-fix` (kebab-case in CLI) - **Python function**: `pr_reconcile_with_remote` → `pr_diverge_fix` (snake_case) - **Slash command**: `/erk:reconcile-with-remote` → `/erk:diverge-fix` - **Output function**: `stream_reconcile_with_remote` → `stream_diverge_fix` - **Result class**: `ReconcileWithRemoteResult` → `DivergeFix­Result` (following frozen dataclass pattern) - **Files to rename**: `reconcile_with_remote_cmd.py` → `diverge_fix_cmd.py` - **Documentation**: `pr-reconcile-with-remote.md` → `pr-diverge-fix.md` - **Test file**: `test_reconcile_with_remote.py` → `test_diverge_fix.py` - **Slash command file**: `.claude/commands/erk/reconcile-with-remote.md` → `.claude/commands/erk/diverge-fix.md`
### Critical Dependencies to Update 1. Import statement in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/__init__.py` (Line 16) 2. Registration in `pr_group.add_command()` (Line 44) 3. Reference in `submit_pipeline.py` error message (Line 297) 4. All docstring references to the command 5. Test imports and invocations 6. Documentation references in `docs/learned/cli/commands/`
### User-Facing Changes - Help text, docstrings, and error messages should reflect new name - Config hint for `require_dangerous_flag_for_implicitly_dangerous_operations` stays the same - Error messages that suggest `erk pr reconcile-with-remote --dangerous` become `erk pr diverge-fix --dangerous`
This is all the information needed to perform a comprehensive rename of the feature.
